title: cyathea subglandulosa
intro: Cyathea subglandulosa, also known as the "New Zealand silver fern," is a species of tree fern native to New Zealand. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Cyathea subglandulosa include its potential to reduce inflammation and improve respiratory health, as well as its ability to support immune function. Cyathea subglandulosa is typically prepared as an herbal tea or extract, and its leaves can also be used in a salve or topical application for skin issues.
